crossFileSnippets.rationale: Find components rendering useAuth to verify UI impact
Find components rendering useAuth to verify UI impact
Find components rendering useAuth to verify UI impact
Find components rendering useAuth to verify UI impact

suggestionsToEvaluate.suggestionContent: The 'user' property was renamed to 'currentUser' (line 55), but ProfileMenu.tsx (src/components/ProfileMenu.tsx) destructures { user } from useAuth(). After this change, 'user' will be undefined, so user.avatarUrl and user.name will throw TypeError at runtime. The null-check 'if (!user)' will always be true, permanently showing LoginButton even for authenticated users.
suggestionsToEvaluate.improvedCode:
user: state.currentUser,
    currentUser: state.currentUser,
suggestionsToEvaluate.oneSentenceSummary: Renamed user->currentUser breaks ProfileMenu which destructures user from useAuth
suggestionsToEvaluate.label: bug

suggestionsToEvaluate.suggestionContent: The 'login' function was renamed to 'signIn' (line 58). This could break callers that still reference the old name. The new name 'signIn' is fine semantically but consumers need to update their imports accordingly.
suggestionsToEvaluate.improvedCode:
login: signIn,
    signIn,
suggestionsToEvaluate.oneSentenceSummary: Renamed login->signIn may break callers still using the old name
suggestionsToEvaluate.label: bug

suggestionsToEvaluate.suggestionContent: The 'isAuthenticated' property was renamed to 'isLoggedIn' (line 57), but ProtectedRoute.tsx (src/components/ProtectedRoute.tsx) destructures { isAuthenticated } from useAuth(). Since isAuthenticated is now undefined (falsy), the route guard will always redirect to /login, locking out all authenticated users from every protected page.
suggestionsToEvaluate.improvedCode:
isAuthenticated: state.isLoggedIn,
    isLoggedIn: state.isLoggedIn,
suggestionsToEvaluate.oneSentenceSummary: Renamed isAuthenticated->isLoggedIn breaks ProtectedRoute which checks isAuthenticated
suggestionsToEvaluate.label: bug

suggestionsToEvaluate.suggestionContent: The 'logout' function was renamed to 'signOut' (line 59), but ProfileMenu.tsx (src/components/ProfileMenu.tsx) destructures { logout } and passes it to the sign-out button's onClick handler. Since 'logout' is now undefined, clicking 'Sign out' calls undefined(), which throws a TypeError.
suggestionsToEvaluate.improvedCode:
logout: signOut,
    signOut,
suggestionsToEvaluate.oneSentenceSummary: Renamed logout->signOut breaks ProfileMenu sign-out button
suggestionsToEvaluate.label: bug

expectedActions.reason: Correctly identifies user->currentUser rename breaking ProfileMenu.tsx, but MISSES UserGreeting.tsx (src/components/UserGreeting.tsx) which also destructures { user } from useAuth(). The suggestion must also list UserGreeting.tsx as an affected caller.

Identifies login->signIn rename as a real bug but describes impact generically ('could break callers') without naming the specific file: LoginForm.tsx (src/components/LoginForm.tsx) which calls login(email, password). Must add the specific file path and concrete impact.

Correctly identifies isAuthenticated->isLoggedIn breaking ProtectedRoute.tsx, but MISSES UserGreeting.tsx (src/components/UserGreeting.tsx) which also destructures { isAuthenticated } from useAuth(). Must list both affected callers.
Correctly identifies logout->signOut rename breaking ProfileMenu.tsx with specific file path and TypeError impact. ProfileMenu is the only caller of logout in the cross-file snippets.

crossFileSnippets.rationale: Find components rendering Button to verify UI impact
Find components rendering Button to verify UI impact
Find components rendering Button to verify UI impact

suggestionsToEvaluate.suggestionContent: The 'onClick' prop was renamed to 'onPress' (line 11). This will break components that still pass 'onClick' to Button, as the handler will be silently ignored. For example, DeleteConfirmDialog.tsx (src/components/DeleteConfirmDialog.tsx) passes onClick={onCancel} and onClick={onConfirm}, so both cancel and delete buttons will be non-functional.
suggestionsToEvaluate.improvedCode:
onClick?: () => void;
  onPress?: () => void;
suggestionsToEvaluate.oneSentenceSummary: Renamed onClick->onPress breaks DeleteConfirmDialog buttons
suggestionsToEvaluate.label: bug

suggestionsToEvaluate.suggestionContent: The 'variant' prop was renamed to 'appearance' (line 9) and its value set changed from 'primary'|'secondary'|'danger'|'ghost' to 'filled'|'outlined'|'ghost'|'link'. Several components pass the old 'variant' prop which will now be silently ignored, causing all buttons to fall back to the default 'filled' appearance. This is a styling regression across the application.
suggestionsToEvaluate.improvedCode:
variant?: ButtonAppearance;
  appearance?: ButtonAppearance;
suggestionsToEvaluate.oneSentenceSummary: Renamed variant->appearance breaks styling across multiple components
suggestionsToEvaluate.label: bug

suggestionsToEvaluate.suggestionContent: The ButtonSize type was changed from 'small'|'medium'|'large' to 'xs'|'sm'|'md'|'lg'|'xl' (lines 5, 10). ToolbarActions.tsx (src/components/ToolbarActions.tsx) passes size="small" to all three of its Button instances, generating the CSS class 'btn-small' which no longer matches any defined style. Similarly, CheckoutPage.tsx (src/pages/CheckoutPage.tsx) passes size="large" which produces 'btn-large' instead of the expected 'btn-lg'. Both components will render buttons with incorrect or missing size styling. This is a TypeScript type error at compile time and a silent CSS mismatch at runtime.
suggestionsToEvaluate.improvedCode: size?: ButtonSize | 'small' | 'medium' | 'large';
suggestionsToEvaluate.oneSentenceSummary: ButtonSize type values changed from small/medium/large to xs/sm/md/lg/xl causing CSS class mismatches in ToolbarActions and CheckoutPage
suggestionsToEvaluate.label: bug

suggestionsToEvaluate.suggestionContent: CheckoutPage.tsx (src/pages/CheckoutPage.tsx) passes onClick={submitOrder} to the Button component. Since Button now only reads 'onPress' (line 11), the Place Order button will render but do nothing when clicked. Users will be unable to complete their purchase, which is a critical business-impacting bug.
suggestionsToEvaluate.oneSentenceSummary: CheckoutPage passes onClick to Button which now only reads onPress, breaking the Place Order button
suggestionsToEvaluate.label: bug

expectedActions.reason: Correctly identifies onClick->onPress rename breaking DeleteConfirmDialog.tsx, but MISSES ToolbarActions.tsx (passes onClick on 3 buttons) and CheckoutPage.tsx (passes onClick={submitOrder}). Must list all three affected files.

Correctly identifies variant->appearance rename as a real bug but describes impact generically ('several components') without listing the specific affected files: DeleteConfirmDialog.tsx (variant='ghost', variant='danger'), ToolbarActions.tsx (variant='secondary', variant='ghost'), and CheckoutPage.tsx (variant='primary'). Must name all three files.
Correctly identifies size enum value changes breaking ToolbarActions and CheckoutPage with specific file paths and CSS class impact.

Correctly identifies onClick->onPress breaking CheckoutPage.tsx but MISSES DeleteConfirmDialog.tsx (2 buttons with onClick) and ToolbarActions.tsx (3 buttons with onClick). Must list all affected files, not just one.

crossFileSnippets.rationale: Find components rendering selectUserProfile to verify UI impact
Find components rendering selectItems to verify UI impact
Find components rendering selectUserSettings to verify UI impact
Find components rendering selectSelectedItem to verify UI impact

suggestionsToEvaluate.suggestionContent: The selector 'selectUserProfile' was renamed to 'selectProfile' (line 88). Components that import selectUserProfile will get a compile-time error since the named export no longer exists. This is a breaking change that needs to be addressed by either keeping the old export or updating all consumers.
suggestionsToEvaluate.existingCode: export const selectProfile = (state: { workspace: WorkspaceState }) => state.workspace.profile;
suggestionsToEvaluate.improvedCode:
export const selectProfile = (state: { workspace: WorkspaceState }) => state.workspace.profile;
export const selectUserProfile = selectProfile;
suggestionsToEvaluate.oneSentenceSummary: Renamed selectUserProfile->selectProfile breaks consumers importing the old name
suggestionsToEvaluate.label: bug

suggestionsToEvaluate.suggestionContent: The selector 'selectUserSettings' was renamed to 'selectPreferences' (line 89), and the state shape changed from nested 'state.workspace.user.settings' to flat 'state.workspace.preferences'. SettingsPanel.tsx (src/components/SettingsPanel.tsx) imports selectUserSettings which no longer exists, causing the settings panel to crash. The theme selector and notifications checkbox will both break.
suggestionsToEvaluate.existingCode: export const selectPreferences = (state: { workspace: WorkspaceState }) => state.workspace.preferences;
suggestionsToEvaluate.improvedCode:
export const selectPreferences = (state: { workspace: WorkspaceState }) => state.workspace.preferences;
export const selectUserSettings = selectPreferences;
suggestionsToEvaluate.oneSentenceSummary: Renamed selectUserSettings->selectPreferences breaks SettingsPanel import
suggestionsToEvaluate.label: bug

suggestionsToEvaluate.suggestionContent: The selector 'selectItems' was renamed to 'selectAllItems' (line 90) and the state changed from a flat array 'state.workspace.items' to a normalized map 'itemsById' with Object.values() conversion. ItemList.tsx (src/components/ItemList.tsx) imports selectItems which no longer exists. The item list component will fail at import time and cannot render any items.
suggestionsToEvaluate.oneSentenceSummary: Renamed selectItems->selectAllItems breaks ItemList import
suggestionsToEvaluate.label: bug

suggestionsToEvaluate.suggestionContent: The selectItemCount selector (line 94) was changed from directly reading state.workspace.itemCount to being derived from selectAllItems via createSelector. This is actually a good improvement -- it removes the redundant 'itemCount' field from state and computes it lazily. The memoized selector ensures the count only recomputes when itemsById changes, avoiding unnecessary re-renders.
suggestionsToEvaluate.improvedCode: export const selectItemCount = createSelector(selectAllItems, (items) => items.length);
suggestionsToEvaluate.oneSentenceSummary: selectItemCount is now properly derived from selectAllItems via createSelector
suggestionsToEvaluate.label: improvement

expectedActions.reason: Correctly identifies selectUserProfile->selectProfile rename as a real bug but describes the impact generically ('components that import') without naming the specific affected file: WorkspaceHeader.tsx (src/components/WorkspaceHeader.tsx) which imports and calls useSelector(selectUserProfile). Must name the specific file.

Correctly identifies selectUserSettings->selectPreferences rename breaking SettingsPanel.tsx, but MISSES WorkspaceHeader.tsx (src/components/WorkspaceHeader.tsx) which also imports selectUserSettings and uses it for the theme indicator. Must list both affected files.

Correctly identifies selectItems->selectAllItems rename breaking ItemList.tsx, but MISSES ItemDetail.tsx (src/components/ItemDetail.tsx) which also imports selectItems for related items filtering. Must list both affected files.
Valid observation that selectItemCount was refactored from a direct state read to a computed selector via createSelector, which is a correct improvement.

crossFileSnippets.rationale: Find components rendering useFormValidation to verify UI impact
Find components rendering useFormValidation to verify UI impact
Find components rendering useFormValidation to verify UI impact

suggestionsToEvaluate.suggestionContent: The hook signature changed from positional arguments 'useFormValidation(rules, validateOnChange, validateOnBlur)' to an options object 'useFormValidation({ rules, ... })' (lines 41-42). ContactForm.tsx (src/components/ContactForm.tsx) calls 'useFormValidation(contactRules, true, true)' passing positional args. The hook will try to destructure 'rules' from the contactRules FieldRules object, which will be undefined, causing all validation to silently fail and making the Contact form submit without any validation.
suggestionsToEvaluate.improvedCode:
export function useFormValidation(rulesOrOptions: FieldRules | UseFormValidationOptions, validateOnChange = true, validateOnBlur = true): UseFormValidationReturn {
  const options = 'rules' in rulesOrOptions ? rulesOrOptions : { rules: rulesOrOptions, validateOnChange, validateOnBlur };
suggestionsToEvaluate.oneSentenceSummary: Positional-to-options signature change breaks ContactForm which passes rules as first positional arg
suggestionsToEvaluate.label: bug

suggestionsToEvaluate.suggestionContent: The return property 'errors' was renamed to 'fieldErrors' (line 30), 'valid' to 'isValid' (line 31), and 'dirty' to 'isDirty' (line 32). RegistrationForm.tsx (src/components/RegistrationForm.tsx) destructures { errors, valid, dirty } from the hook. After this change, all three will be undefined: the submit button's 'disabled={!valid || !dirty}' evaluates to '!undefined || !undefined' which is true, permanently disabling the register button.
suggestionsToEvaluate.oneSentenceSummary: Renamed errors/valid/dirty to fieldErrors/isValid/isDirty breaks RegistrationForm destructuring
suggestionsToEvaluate.label: bug

suggestionsToEvaluate.suggestionContent: The 'reset' method was renamed to 'resetValidation' (line 35). This will break form components that call the old 'reset()' method. Callers need to update their destructuring to use the new name.
suggestionsToEvaluate.improvedCode:
reset: () => void;
  resetValidation: () => void;
suggestionsToEvaluate.oneSentenceSummary: Renamed reset->resetValidation breaks callers using the old method name
suggestionsToEvaluate.label: bug

suggestionsToEvaluate.suggestionContent: The 'firstError' method was renamed to 'getFieldError' (line 38). RegistrationForm.tsx (src/components/RegistrationForm.tsx) calls 'firstError("email")' to display validation errors below the email input and also uses it in conditional rendering. Since firstError is now undefined, calling it will throw TypeError, crashing the entire registration form component.
suggestionsToEvaluate.improvedCode:
firstError: (field: string) => string | undefined;
  getFieldError: (field: string) => string | undefined;
suggestionsToEvaluate.oneSentenceSummary: Renamed firstError->getFieldError crashes RegistrationForm which calls firstError('email')
suggestionsToEvaluate.label: bug

suggestionsToEvaluate.suggestionContent: The 'touch' method was renamed to 'touchField' and 'isTouched' to 'isFieldTouched' (lines 36-37). ContactForm.tsx (src/components/ContactForm.tsx) calls 'touch(field)' in its handleBlur handler and 'isTouched("name")' for conditional error display. Since both are now undefined, the blur handler throws TypeError and the error display logic never shows validation errors, even when the user leaves an invalid field.
suggestionsToEvaluate.existingCode:
touchField: (field: string) => void;
  isFieldTouched: (field: string) => boolean;
suggestionsToEvaluate.oneSentenceSummary: Renamed touch->touchField and isTouched->isFieldTouched breaks ContactForm blur handler and error display
suggestionsToEvaluate.label: bug

expectedActions.reason: Correctly identifies positional-to-options signature change breaking ContactForm.tsx, but MISSES RegistrationForm.tsx (calls useFormValidation(registrationRules) with positional arg) and ProfileEditForm.tsx (calls useFormValidation(profileRules, false) with positional args). Must list all three affected callers.

Correctly identifies errors/valid/dirty renames breaking RegistrationForm.tsx, but MISSES ContactForm.tsx (destructures { errors, valid }) and ProfileEditForm.tsx (destructures { errors, valid, dirty }). Must list all three affected forms.

Correctly identifies reset->resetValidation rename as a real bug but describes impact generically ('form components that call the old reset()') without naming the specific files: RegistrationForm.tsx (calls reset), ContactForm.tsx (calls reset()), and ProfileEditForm.tsx (calls reset() in useEffect). Must name all three files.

Correctly identifies firstError->getFieldError rename breaking RegistrationForm.tsx, but MISSES ProfileEditForm.tsx (src/components/ProfileEditForm.tsx) which also calls firstError('name'). Must list both affected files.

Correctly identifies touch->touchField and isTouched->isFieldTouched renames breaking ContactForm.tsx with specific file path and both the blur handler and error display impact. ContactForm is the only caller of these methods in the cross-file snippets.

crossFileSnippets.rationale: Find components rendering useTheme to verify UI impact
Find components rendering useTheme to verify UI impact
Find components rendering useTheme to verify UI impact
Find components rendering useTheme to verify UI impact
Find components rendering useTheme to verify UI impact

suggestionsToEvaluate.suggestionContent: The 'colors.primary' token was renamed to 'colors.brand' (line 6), along with 'primaryHover' -> 'brandHover' and 'primaryLight' -> 'brandLight'. PrimaryButton.tsx (src/components/PrimaryButton.tsx) uses 'theme.colors.primary' for its backgroundColor, which is now undefined. The button will render with no background color (transparent), making it invisible or unreadable against the page background.
suggestionsToEvaluate.oneSentenceSummary: Renamed colors.primary->brand breaks PrimaryButton background color rendering
suggestionsToEvaluate.label: bug

suggestionsToEvaluate.suggestionContent: The spacing tokens were renamed from short names ('xs', 'sm', 'md', 'lg', 'xl') to full names ('xsmall', 'small', 'medium', 'large', 'xlarge') on lines 19-23. PrimaryButton.tsx (src/components/PrimaryButton.tsx) uses 'theme.spacing.sm' and 'theme.spacing.md' for padding, and Card.tsx (src/components/Card.tsx) uses 'theme.spacing.lg'. These references return undefined, collapsing all padding in these components.
suggestionsToEvaluate.oneSentenceSummary: Renamed spacing tokens xs/sm/md/lg/xl to xsmall/small/medium/large/xlarge breaks PrimaryButton and Card padding
suggestionsToEvaluate.label: bug

suggestionsToEvaluate.suggestionContent: The breakpoints field was changed from a named object with keys 'mobile', 'tablet', 'desktop', 'wide' to a positional tuple type '[string, string, string, string]' (line 25 of new hunk). ResponsiveGrid.tsx (src/components/ResponsiveGrid.tsx) uses 'theme.breakpoints.tablet' and 'theme.breakpoints.desktop' in template literal strings to build CSS @media queries. Since the breakpoints value is now an array, accessing named properties like '.tablet' or '.desktop' on it yields undefined, producing invalid CSS rules '@media (min-width: undefined)'. The responsive grid layout will remain single-column on all screen sizes.
suggestionsToEvaluate.improvedCode: breakpoints: { mobile: string; tablet: string; desktop: string; wide: string };
suggestionsToEvaluate.oneSentenceSummary: Breakpoints changed from named object to positional tuple breaks ResponsiveGrid CSS media query generation
suggestionsToEvaluate.label: bug

suggestionsToEvaluate.suggestionContent: The 'fonts.body' token was renamed to 'fonts.sans' (line 27). This change will affect components that use 'theme.fonts.body' for fontFamily, as the property is now undefined. The affected components will fall back to the browser's default serif font instead of the intended sans-serif font.
suggestionsToEvaluate.improvedCode:
body: string;
    sans: string;
suggestionsToEvaluate.oneSentenceSummary: Renamed fonts.body->fonts.sans breaks font rendering in components using theme.fonts.body
suggestionsToEvaluate.label: bug

suggestionsToEvaluate.suggestionContent: The shadow tokens were renamed: 'shadows.card' -> 'shadows.elevation1', 'shadows.dropdown' -> 'shadows.elevation2' (lines 32-34). Card.tsx (src/components/Card.tsx) uses 'theme.shadows.card' for its box shadow, which is now undefined -- cards render flat with no shadow. DropdownMenu.tsx (src/components/DropdownMenu.tsx) uses 'theme.shadows.dropdown' which is also undefined, so dropdown menus appear without any shadow or visual separation from the background.
suggestionsToEvaluate.oneSentenceSummary: Renamed shadows.card/dropdown to elevation1/elevation2 breaks Card and DropdownMenu box shadows
suggestionsToEvaluate.label: bug

expectedActions.reason: Correctly identifies colors.primary->brand rename breaking PrimaryButton.tsx with specific file path and visual impact. PrimaryButton is the only consumer of colors.primary in the cross-file snippets.

Correctly identifies spacing token renames breaking PrimaryButton.tsx and Card.tsx, but MISSES ResponsiveGrid.tsx (uses theme.spacing.md and theme.spacing.lg), DropdownMenu.tsx (uses theme.spacing.xs and theme.spacing.sm), and FormField.tsx (uses theme.spacing.md and theme.spacing.xs). Must list all 5 affected components.

Correctly identifies breakpoints type change from named object to array breaking ResponsiveGrid media queries with specific file path. ResponsiveGrid is the only consumer of named breakpoint properties.

Correctly identifies fonts.body->sans rename as a real bug but describes impact generically ('components that use theme.fonts.body') without naming the specific files: PrimaryButton.tsx, DropdownMenu.tsx, and FormField.tsx all use theme.fonts.body. Must list all three affected files. Severity should also be critical, not medium, given 3 components break.
Correctly identifies shadows.card and shadows.dropdown renames breaking Card.tsx and DropdownMenu.tsx with specific file paths and visual impact.
